nodes.label: Nouns and Pronouns
nodes.description: Naming words and the words that replace them

nodes.label: Nouns
nodes.description: Words that name a person, place, animal, or thing

nodes.label: Common Nouns
nodes.description: General names like boy, city, dog

nodes.label: Proper Nouns
nodes.description: Special names that start with a capital letter like India, Riya

nodes.label: Collective Nouns
nodes.description: Names for groups like a flock of birds, a team of players

nodes.label: Abstract Nouns
nodes.description: Names for feelings and ideas like happiness, honesty

nodes.label: Singular & Plural
nodes.description: One noun vs. more than one: cat/cats, box/boxes

nodes.label: Pronouns
nodes.description: Words used in place of nouns to avoid repetition

nodes.label: Subject Pronouns
nodes.description: I, you, he, she, it, we, they - do the action

nodes.label: Object Pronouns
nodes.description: me, you, him, her, it, us, them - receive the action

nodes.label: Why Use Pronouns?
nodes.description: To avoid repeating the same noun again and again

nodes.label: Nouns & Pronouns Together
nodes.description: Pronouns always refer back to a noun in the sentence or paragraph

nodes.label: Example Sentences
nodes.description: Ravi plays cricket. He is very good at it.

nodes.label: Remember!
nodes.description: A pronoun must match the noun it replaces in number and gender
